exploration_log.aha_moment: ADO.NET `ExecuteNonQuery` returns -1 for stored procedures that produce intermediate result sets (row counts from internal DML). Using `ExecuteReader()` and checking `RecordsAffected` at each `NextResult()` gives access to all intermediate row counts.
exploration_log.hindsight: Should double-check whether RecordsAffected is cumulative or per-result-set in different providers. The implementation checks both per-step and after the loop to be safe.

alternatives: Wrap ExecuteNonQuery in a try-catch and fall back on -1
Use a DataReader to iterate all result sets and sum RecordsAffected
key_decisions.decision: When CommandType is StoredProcedure, use ExecuteReader() and accumulate RecordsAffected across all NextResult() calls
key_decisions.reasoning: Only ExecuteReader allows iterating through all result sets to collect intermediate row counts; only applies to stored procedures to avoid overhead for regular queries.

search_sequence.result: Found single cmd.ExecuteNonQuery() call at line 2994 with no special handling for stored procedures

solve_notes: Modified `ExecuteCommand` to detect when the command is a `StoredProcedure` and switch from `ExecuteNonQuery` (which returns -1 when intermediate result sets exist) to `ExecuteReader`, iterating through all result sets with `NextResult()` and summing non-negative `RecordsAffected` values.
task_text:
Fix Execute returning -1 for stored procedures with no SET NOCOUNT

`Execute()` returns `-1` for stored procedure calls when the procedure
does not set `NOCOUNT ON`, because the intermediate row counts from
DML statements inside the procedure confuse the result aggregation.
Fix `SqlMapper.cs` to aggregate all `RecordsAffected` values from the
command execution for stored-procedure commands.

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set is just `ExecuteCommand` (the only edited method). `ExecuteImpl` is added to thrash_preventing because an AI agent needs to understand the call chain and see how `command.CommandTypeDirect` is used before reaching `ExecuteCommand`. `CommandTypeDirect` in CommandDefinition.cs is added because an agent would look up its exact semantics before using it.
